persona.name: Amara Okafor
persona.role: Data Engineer — Federal Skilled Worker (STEM) applicant
education: MSc Computer Science, University of Lagos, 2017
BSc Mathematics, University of Nigeria Nsukka, 2015

brother.name: Chukwuemeka (Emeka) Okafor
brother.status: Final-year medical student, University of Ibadan

career.company_description: Nigerian fintech company — processes 500M+ transactions annually, powers Verve cards across Africa
career.specialization: Data pipeline architecture, real-time fraud detection, Apache Kafka/Spark, Python, SQL, cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP)
notable_projects: Built real-time fraud detection pipeline processing 2M transactions/day
Migrated legacy batch ETL to streaming architecture — cut processing time from 6 hours to 12 minutes
Data infrastructure for Quickteller mobile payments (40M users)

immigration.stream: Express Entry — Federal Skilled Worker Program (STEM category-based draw)
immigration.crs_problem: General FSW draws stopped in 2025. All draws are category-based. STEM draws have CRS cutoffs of 481-491. She is 11 points short.
immigration.crs_strategy: TEF French test. CLB 7 French adds 37 CRS points through SOL points (12) and bilingual bonus (25). Her secondary school French is the foundation.
immigration.eca: WES — evaluated MSc as equivalent to Canadian master's degree

financial.savings: $25,000 CAD (settlement funds — minimum for FSWP is $15,263 for single applicant)
financial.note: This is the affordable program. No $350K investment. No $15K startup fees. Just the application cost ($1,525 government fees) + consultant retainer.

persona.voice: Direct, analytical, quietly determined. First in her family to have a master's degree. Carries the weight of being the eldest — mother's retirement, brother's tuition. Doesn't complain about the system, just wants to understand the rules so she can win. Grew up writing code on a shared computer at a cybercafe in Enugu. Now builds systems that process millions of transactions. Skeptical of consultants — she's seen too many scams targeting Nigerians. Needs to trust Chris before she'll pay. Thinks in data: probabilities, thresholds, cutoff scores. The CRS system is just another algorithm to optimize.
priorities: Understand why general draws stopped and when STEM draws happen
The 11-point CRS gap — what's the most reliable way to close it?
French TEF preparation — how long, how hard, what score needed?
Settlement funds — is $25K enough for Toronto until first paycheck?
Job search strategy — she has no Canadian employer and no connections
Timeline — how long from Express Entry profile to PR card in hand?
concerns: What if STEM draws stop too? What if IRCC changes the categories again?
Is $3,000-6,000 for a consultant worth it when the FSWP application is self-serve?
She's seen Nigerian 'immigration agents' steal people's savings — how is Chris different?
Her salary is $16K CAD — the consultant fee is 25% of her annual income
What happens if she lands in Toronto with no job? How long can $25K last?
Her mother needs her. If this fails, she's spent 3 years of savings on nothing.
She doesn't know anyone in Toronto except a university friend who moved 2 years ago
persona.why_this_story_matters: Ahmed had a startup. Priya had $800K. Jun-seo had a job offer from Sanctuary AI. Amara has nothing except her skills, her scores, and $25,000 she saved over 3 years while supporting her family. FSWP is the program for people who don't have connections or capital — just talent and determination. If the platform works for Amara, it works for everyone.

narrator.voice: Third-person. The pure skilled worker perspective — no employer, no investment, no provincial backing. Just the points system and a person who has been preparing for this her entire adult life. This is immigration at its most democratic and its most brutal.
